posts.name: Andrzej Novak
posts.text: I have a workspace which is yielding negative saturated GoF values for some toys when running with --toysFr (unfortunately I don’t have an easy reproducer, since I don’t know what’s causing the issue). Are there any pointers to how that could happen? I was under the impression that there’s no way for saturated to be negative
posts.cooked_html: I have a workspace which is yielding negative saturated GoF values for some toys when running with --toysFr (unfortunately I don’t have an easy reproducer, since I don’t know what’s causing the issue). Are there any pointers to how that could happen? I was under the impression that there’s no way for saturated to be negative

posts.name: Aliya Nigamova
posts.text:
Hi Andrzej,
Do you observe it for majority of the toys or just a few of them?
The saturated test statistics values are not restricted to be positive, and I think it can happen that they go negative if the fits are failing for example. I think you should see some error messages already for those cases, but you can try to  increase verbosity adding -v 3.
Best,
Aliya
posts.cooked_html:
